Description: Uncovered a GitHub Personal Access Token, potentially leading to unauthorized repository access and sensitive content exposure.
Author: mattg-stack
Message:
feat(taps): Claude Code session-transcript Tap — GitHub #80

Ingests ~/.claude/projects/*/<session_uuid>.jsonl conversation
transcripts into pgvector so ``mcp__poindexter__search_memory`` can
retrieve past reasoning + decisions, not just curated memory files.

**Implementation** (\`services/taps/claude_code_sessions.py\`):

- Streams each JSONL file line-by-line (sessions hit 30+ MB)
- Filters noise: file-history-snapshot, system events, thinking blocks,
  system-reminder + command-name tags, autonomous-loop sentinels
- Summarizes tool_use blocks as \`[tool: Name(arg_summary)]\`
- Truncates tool_result bodies past config \`max_tool_result_chars\`
- Scrubs credentials pre-embed: sk-..., sk-ant-..., ghp_...,
  github_pat_..., AKIA..., JWT, xox[baprs]-..., and our own
  \`enc:v1:<base64>\` ciphertext prefix
- Pre-chunks sessions at USER:/ASSISTANT: boundaries with a 3500-char
  cap (below nomic-embed-text's 8192-token limit for dense technical
  content). Multi-part Documents get \`/part-NNN\` suffixed source_ids

**Config (plugin.tap.claude_code_sessions)**:

- \`claude_projects_dir\` — override default \`~/.claude/projects\`
- \`max_sessions_per_run\` — cap per cycle (0 = unlimited)
- \`session_age_max_days\` — skip sessions older than N days (0 = no cap)
- \`max_tool_result_chars\` — tool output truncation threshold (default 2000)
- \`extra_scrub_patterns\` — additional regex patterns for site-specific
  credential formats

**Registration**:

- Entry point: \`poindexter.taps.claude_code_sessions\`
- Imperative fallback: added to \`plugins/registry.py::get_core_samples\`
  matching the other core taps (entry_points disabled in current build)

**Tests** (46 total):
- Scrub: 8 cases — every pattern + innocent text + custom extras
- Noise filters: system reminders, command-name, autonomous-loop
- Extraction: user (string + array content), assistant (text/thinking/
  tool_use/tool_result), tool-input summary truncation
- Rendering: mixed events, empty session, malformed JSON lines
- Chunking: short session one-chunk, long session at boundaries,
  oversized single turn hard-sliced, blank-chunk filter
- Integration: multi-scope discovery, max_sessions cap, age filter,
  secret scrub end-to-end, missing projects dir graceful skip
- Tap contract: required attrs + async generator

**Live validation** (post auto-embed rebuild):
- 5127 chunks embedded on first pass, 0 failures
- 6041 total claude_sessions rows in pgvector after cycle
- Covers all 1030 session files across 4 scope directories
- Previously-failing large sessions (fcfc38c2, 2ff11139, d4eb3c42,
  d1408f2b — each 20+ MB) now embed cleanly via pre-chunking

Closes GitHub #80.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Author: mattg-stack
Message:
docs: Day 1 of the public/premium doc split — move + rebrand pass

Lands ~2,500 lines of mastery-grade technical documentation into
the public repo under docs/, moved from the private glad-labs-prompts
repo per the Day 1 plan in docs/DOC-SPLIT-PLAN.md.

Files added (moved from glad-labs-prompts/docs/, then rebranded from
the old "AI Co-Founder" / "Glad Labs Engine" framing to Poindexter):

- docs/README.md                           — the doc index (new)
- docs/ARCHITECTURE.md                     — 715 lines, rewrote the
                                             Vision/Mission section to
                                             match current positioning
                                             ("quality automated content
                                             with human oversight"), fixed
                                             port 8000 → 8002, dropped
                                             Railway deploy references
- docs/architecture/multi-agent-pipeline.md  — 572 lines, rebranded +
                                             replaced Railway deploy
                                             section with docker-compose
- docs/architecture/database-schema.md     — 273 lines, light rebrand
- docs/api/README.md                       — 264 lines, rewrote header
                                             with current port + stability
                                             warning
- docs/operations/local-development-setup.md — rewrote from scratch
                                             (was a 39-line stub); now
                                             ~140 lines covering all 10
                                             containers + verification
- docs/operations/environment-variables.md — rewrote from scratch (was
                                             based on stale Railway
                                             GitHub Actions flow); now
                                             accurately lists the env
                                             vars docker-compose.local.yml
                                             actually reads, explicitly
                                             points at app_settings table
                                             for runtime config
- docs/operations/ci-deploy-chain.md       — 89 lines, reframed as
                                             "how Poindexter itself is
                                             tested and deployed" (a
                                             transparency record for
                                             contributors) rather than
                                             "how to set up your own CI"
- docs/operations/troubleshooting.md       — 173 lines, light rebrand
                                             (gladlabs-worker →
                                             poindexter-worker)
- docs/feature-status.md                   — 177 lines, header only

Also updated the top-level README.md to link into the new docs/
tree so GitHub visitors see the full technical surface area
instead of an empty docs/ or a README-only repo.

The outline-only docs/quick-start-guide-outline.md is NOT tracked —
it duplicates content that stays premium.

After this commit the public repo has ~2,700 lines of real technical
reference material on top of the existing README. Still TODO for
Day 2+: reference catalogs (app_settings, prompt_templates, QA
pipeline, validators), extension guides, API endpoint reference.
See docs/DOC-SPLIT-PLAN.md for the full roadmap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Message:

Backend auth fixes:
- Gate get_current_user_optional dev bypass on DEVELOPMENT_MODE=true (#276)
- Gate get_user_id() "test-user-123" fallback on DEVELOPMENT_MODE=true (#285)
- Gate DISABLE_AUTH_FOR_DEV bypass on ENVIRONMENT != production (#292)
- Add router-level auth to entire /api/workflows router (#296)
- Add auth to task metrics endpoints (#382)
- Replace mid-request DatabaseService() with pool injection in cms_routes (#264)
- Fix Poetry lockfile: pin pytest-benchmark to ^4.0 (#395)

Frontend XSS fixes:
- PostEditor.jsx: wrap dangerouslySetInnerHTML with DOMPurify.sanitize() (#305)
- posts/[slug]/page.tsx: sanitize post.content with sanitize-html before render (#313)

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: mattg-stack
Message:
fix: Content generation pipeline TypeError - NoneType has no len()

Root Cause:
- Line 475 in content_router_service.py was calling get_seo_content_generator()
  without required arguments
- Function signature requires 'ai_content_generator' parameter
- Was also being awaited incorrectly (not an async function)
- This caused seo_generator to be None, leading to TypeError when accessing methods

Fix:
- Pass content_generator as argument: get_seo_content_generator(content_generator)
- Remove incorrect 'await' keyword
- Now seo_generator is properly initialized before use

Impact:
- Fixes 'object of type NoneType has no len()' error in task result preview
- Content generation pipeline can now proceed to Stage 4 (SEO metadata generation)
- Closes issue where tasks were being marked as deleted with no content

Author: mattg-stack
Message:
feat(auth): Phase 1.1 - Database schema and ORM models complete

PHASE 1.1 IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE
=================================

Implemented foundational database infrastructure for Settings Management and Authentication system:

DELIVERABLES (2,100+ lines of production code):

1. SQLAlchemy ORM Models (models.py - 580 lines)
   - 10 complete models: User, Role, Permission, RolePermission, UserRole, Session, Setting, SettingAuditLog, FeatureFlag, APIKey
   - 30+ indexes for performance
   - 50+ constraints (unique, check, foreign key)
   - Validators for data integrity
   - Relationships with cascade rules

2. Database Connection Module (database.py - 450 lines)
   - PostgreSQL engine with connection pooling (20 pool, 40 overflow)
   - Session factory with context managers
   - init_db() for schema creation
   - seed_initial_data() creating 4 system roles + 13 permissions
   - Health checks and flexible environment configuration
   - SQLite fallback for local development

3. Encryption Service (encryption.py - 520 lines)
   - AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption (detects tampering)
   - PBKDF2-SHA256 password hashing with 480,000 iterations (OWASP 2023 standard)
   - Cryptographically secure API key generation
   - Master key from DATABASE_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable
   - Singleton pattern with singleton accessor

4. Alembic Migration (001_initial_schema.py - 550 lines)
   - Complete database schema creation
   - 10 tables with full DDL
   - 20+ unique constraints, 15+ check constraints
   - 30+ foreign keys with cascade deletes
   - 30+ indexes for query optimization
   - Downgrade support for rollback

ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS:

 Cost Optimization: Single database (no new infrastructure cost)
 Security: AES-256-GCM encryption + PBKDF2-SHA256 + immutable audit logs
 Scalability: Connection pooling, environment-based configuration
 Developer Experience: Clean ORM, context managers, type hints, comprehensive docs

RBAC SEEDED:

4 System Roles:
- ADMIN: Full access
- MANAGER: read/write/delete on settings and users
- OPERATOR: read/write on settings
- VIEWER: read-only access

13 Base Permissions:
- settings: read, write, delete, admin
- users: read, write, delete, admin
- audit: read, admin
- roles: read, write, admin

READY FOR NEXT PHASE:

Phase 1.2 will implement:
- JWT authentication endpoints
- TOTP 2FA support
- Rate limiting middleware
- Session management with Redis

Run: alembic upgrade head
to create the schema in your PostgreSQL database.
